purpose: No-secret decision matrix for selecting the next approved private workflow target after the Firebase handoff pilot. This file must not contain provider URLs, private account details, private endpoint paths, private values, tokens, or credentials.
decisionRequiredBefore: Replacing firebase_handoff as the primary receiver, activating a future profile, or routing public capture records into a third-party workflow.
activationBoundary: All external targets remain server-side only. Store receiver location and signing material in Firebase Secret Manager, never in public markup, client JavaScript, docs, or screenshots.
operatorPrinciple: Choose the first external target by the job it must do: follow-up, archive, review packet, or operator triage. Do not choose by brand familiarity alone.
commandTemplateNote: Command receiver placeholders are intentionally not runnable from this file. Replace them with approved private values outside the repo.

candidates.label: Firebase Private Handoff
bestFor: handoff replay before external workflow selection
prerequisites: Firebase Functions and Firestore remain active
Approved operator report token remains configured
approvalGuards: No future-profile approval required while this remains the active MVP receiver
acceptance: Provider readiness reports private handoff active
Live operator report shows handoff target firebase_handoff
Pending handoffs can be replayed from the private report

candidates.label: Approved Private Workflow
bestFor: A human-approved receiver that is not yet provider-specific
private automation routing behind one screened endpoint
temporary bridge while GHL, Workspace, or CodexOps details are finalized
notFor: storing private provider setup details in this repo
prerequisites: Human approval of the receiver owner
Field map accepted by the workflow owner
approvalGuards: Requires private receiver approval before secret update
Requires dry run before writing Firebase secrets
acceptance: Provider readiness reports the target active after activation
Live operator report shows the chosen target and no secret-shaped values

candidates.label: GoHighLevel CRM
bestFor: tags and pipeline stages for screened capture
prerequisites: Approved CRM account and operator owner
Consent language reviewed for email and SMS paths
Tag and pipeline naming approved outside this repo
Receiver confirms idempotency and replay behavior
approvalGuards: Requires Firebase Secret Manager setup only
acceptance: One live form matrix write reaches inbox and private handoff after activation
Operator report shows CRM target without exposing provider configuration

candidates.label: Google Workspace Intake
bestFor: review rows for submissions and strategic access
prerequisites: Approved private Drive or Workspace destination
Access controls reviewed by the business owner
Receiver confirms idempotency and replay behavior
approvalGuards: Requires Firebase Secret Manager setup only
acceptance: Private workflow matrix owner accepts queue routing
Operator report shows Workspace target without exposing destination details

candidates.label: CodexOps Bridge
prerequisites: Packet fields reviewed against the public product boundary
Human review step assigned before any generated action
Receiver confirms idempotency and replay behavior
approvalGuards: Requires Firebase Secret Manager setup only
acceptance: No private prompts or internal dashboards are routed through the public site
Operator report shows CodexOps target without exposing private workspace details

queueDecisions.reason: Membership and first-access leads benefit from tags, follow-up stages, and screened nurture.
Submissions need review records, legal-version context, and a rights-safe packet path before automation.
Event interest needs timely follow-up, invite status, and list segmentation.
Press needs a controlled response log and public-kit request routing before automated nurture.
Partner and licensing inquiries need screened review context before any CRM or build-packet routing.
Investor and strategic access should stay review-led and should not expose data-room material.
Portal access requests need role review before later auth, room, or internal bridge work.

approvalSequence: Confirm Privacy and Terms legal review status.
Choose one target profile and one workflow owner.
Export the private integration activation request.
Approve the receiver location and signing material outside this repo.
Run activation dry run for the selected target.
Set Firebase secrets only after approval.
Deploy functions and run provider readiness.
Run one live form write matrix only when the inbox owner is ready for QA mail.
Replay pending handoffs from the protected operator report if needed.
rollback: Restore the Firebase receiver location and signing material through Firebase Secret Manager.
Run provider readiness and live operator report QA.
